what_it_does: This file is a large, monolithic CSS stylesheet for the ConceptV Workshop app. It lays out the document/slide editor chrome (picker, library, outline, workspace), defines a fixed 1280x720 slide canvas that scales to fit its container, supplies a wide catalog of slide/content block styles, and adds UI for widget/wizard builders, an AI candidate gallery, diff cards, suggestions, onboarding, shortcuts, and error boundaries.
observations: Uses a warm gold/bronze token palette via CSS custom properties (var(--accent-gold), var(--accent-bronze), var(--bg-surface), etc.).
Implements a fixed 16:9 slide stage (1280x720) scaled with transform-origin: top left so block layouts do not reflow at narrow widths.

Provides many reusable block patterns: hero, headline, quote, stats, process, funnel, tag cluster, pull quote, metric row, bullets, icon strip, labeled card, comparison, hex badges, chip row, stat pills/table, checklist, logo strip, bio, timeline, pillars, photobio, org diagram, contact card.
Themes slides via data-attributes: data-bleed="dark" and data-bleed="gold" invert or recolor content.
Includes drag-and-drop affordances: cursor: grab/grabbing, .drop-active, .drag-over-top/bot, .dragging opacity.
Contains a full Widget Builder section with kind/system/mode pickers, a two/three-column workspace, context frames (dashboard, hub, embed), and a variations panel.
Contains a Wizard Builder section with sidebar/progress/cards layouts, step lists, choice cards, review rows, and celebration state.
Contains an AI Engine section: candidate gallery modal, skeleton states, mini-previews for blocks/pages/widgets/steps, diff cards, suggestion rail, field toolbars, and transform menus.
Includes polish/auxiliary UI: shortcuts overlay, onboarding tour, error boundary, embed picker thumbnails, and inline editable focus rings.
Uses a few media queries and hardcoded scale ratios (e.g., thumbnail scale 0.125).
standouts: The scaled fixed-ratio slide canvas technique (1280px frame scaled into a responsive stage).
The extensive catalog of slide block patterns and motif backgrounds, all themeable through data-attributes.
The AI candidate gallery with skeletons, mini-previews, diff cards, and inline field toolbars.
The Widget/Wizard builder UI with dashboard, hub, and embed context frames.
conventions: BEM-ish prefixed class names: .ws-* for workshop components.
Heavy reliance on design-system CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radius, shadow, and typography.
Section comments mark major areas (/* ===== Block content styles ===== */).
Sticky side panels with max-height: calc(100vh - offset).
Use of color-mix(in oklch, ...) for tinted surfaces.
concerns: The file is very long and monolithic, mixing document editor, slide builder, widget/wizard builder, and AI UI in one sheet.
Some selectors are highly specific and chained (e.g., .ws-slide-frame[data-bleed="dark"] .ws-blk-...).
The polish pass uses !important in several places, which may complicate overrides.
Slide and thumbnail scaling via transform: scale() can create inaccessible hit targets and may require JS to keep in sync.
Animations (pulse, shimmer, fade) do not appear to respect prefers-reduced-motion.
notes: A dense, token-driven stylesheet that tries to cover nearly every visual surface of the Workshop app in one file.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties (var(--accent-gold), etc.) defined?
What JavaScript drives the drag-and-drop, AI candidate generation, and slide scaling calculations?
What do the comment references like image_13, image_14, and sshot_113712 refer to?
Is the .dsa-* class family (e.g., dsa-btn, dsa-msg) defined in another file?
How are block types mapped to the HTML structure and the layout picker?
What is the exact relationship between Workshop and Studio mentioned in the header?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says it extends Studio's design language for a workshop canvas. The file does that, but its actual scope is much broader: it also implements builder chrome, widget/wizard contexts, and AI assistant surfaces.
intention_signals: It appears designed to be the single visual substrate for a multi-mode creative workshop where users assemble documents, slides, widgets, and wizards, while an AI assistant proposes variations, diffs, and suggestions inside the same design language.
novelty: The combination of a scaled fixed-ratio slide canvas, an extensive motif/theme inversion system, a large library of editorial block patterns, and integrated AI UI (candidate gallery, diff cards, field toolbars) in one CSS file.
contribution: It contributes a rich, token-driven visual vocabulary and interaction affordances for a Workshop app: slide scaling layout, themeable block patterns, builder panels, widget/wizard context frames, and AI assistant surfaces that a fused system would keep as the design substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A large CSS stylesheet for the ConceptV Workshop app defining layout grids, a scaled fixed-ratio slide canvas, motif themes, dozens of content block styles, drag-and-drop panels, widget/wizard builder chrome, and AI-engine UI components.
intention_for_embedding: To extend Studio's design language into a WYSIWYG workshop surface for documents, slides, widgets, wizards, and AI-assisted content editing.
